column2: export const DEFAULT_MEMORY_FLUSH_SOFT_TOKENS = 4000; This gives the agent headroom to save memories before the hard limit triggers compaction. Context compaction is inevitable. The question is whether you lose information or save it first. The flush prompt turns a destructive operation into a checkpoint— memories survive because the system asks before it forgets. 4. Embedding Providers: Local First, Cloud Fallback OpenClaw tries local embeddings first, then falls back to cloud providers. You might have a local model, or an OpenAI key, or just Gemini—it figures out what's available.
